titleStmt.title: Letter from Joseph Slevin to the Longford Urban District Council, 3 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Joseph Slevin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Joseph Slevin to the Longford Urban District Council in which Slevin submits his Towns Surveyor report. Joseph Slevin (b. 1856) was the Town Surveyor for Longford. The Longford Urban District Council was the towns local authority and was renamed the Longford Town Council in 2002.

correspAction.placeName: Longford Urban District Council, Longford, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Town Surveyor &questionable reading Officers Report Mr Chairman & Gentlemen I beg to report that I have had a house
in Blue Yard disinfected and some bedding
etc. burned for which I append a bill. Mr Martin closed a drain that took the
overflow from Quarry and then wanted to
prevent the men working unless a pipe
drain is made.. I told them not to mind him
but go on with their work but we shall
probably hear more about it — I consider the grate in Mr Friars house
St. Niels Road could be reset and new
concrete hearth put in for about 10/- Your obdt Servant
J Slevin 3:7:16
